subtitle: the Divine Comedy — down through the Nine Circles, and out to the stars
author: Dante Alighieri
premise: Lost at the midpoint of life in a dark wood, a Florentine poet is led down through the Nine Circles of Hell by the shade of Virgil, up the Mount of Purgatory, and at last to Paradise by Beatrice. Hell is a machine of perfect justice: every soul sorted to the circle its sin has earned. This roll is that machine — the damned by their circle, the guides who pass through, and the frozen traitor at the dead center of the world.
note: All three books of the Comedy (Inferno, Purgatorio, Paradiso) form the lineage; Inferno's nine circles are the spine of the roll. Lucifer is set apart at the bottom. Every figure is an ACI carrying the full DLW tag — .agent · .png · .spun (who/what/where/why/when/how) · .1099 — and the repo carries .attribute. Characters by Dante Alighieri; the darkest punishments are treated as archetype, not detail.

classes.label: Lucifer
classes.spec: the frozen center of the world

members.name: Dante
members.who: A Florentine poet, lost at the midpoint of life in a dark wood.
members.what: The pilgrim who walks down through Hell, up the Mount, and home to the stars.
members.where: From the dark wood to the Empyrean — through all three realms.
members.why: To be saved, and to bring back the map for everyone else.
members.how: Led first by reason, then by grace, one trembling foot before the next.

members.name: Virgil
members.who: The poet of Rome, author of the Aeneid.
members.what: Dante's guide through Hell and Purgatory — reason itself, given a voice.
members.where: He dwells in Limbo, and walks the pilgrim down and up.
members.why: Sent by Beatrice to lead a living soul as far as reason can go.
members.how: With calm authority — and the quiet grief of a virtuous pagan who cannot follow to Heaven.

members.name: Beatrice
members.who: The woman Dante loved, now blessed among the saints.
members.what: Divine love and grace; the guide through Paradise.
members.where: The Empyrean — the white rose of the blessed.
members.why: Love that moves the sun and the other stars, reaching down to save him.
members.when: From beyond death, at the journey's far end.
members.how: She sends Virgil first, then leads where Virgil cannot.

members.name: Homer
members.who: The sovereign poet of the Greeks.
members.what: Greatest of the virtuous pagans, honored in the noble castle of Limbo.
members.why: Born before the faith that might have saved him, through no fault of his own.
members.when: In a green twilight without end.
members.how: He sinned not, and is denied still — sorrow without torment.

members.name: Aristotle
members.what: The philosopher all the others defer to, in Limbo's castle.
members.why: Reason perfected — but reason alone cannot reach Heaven.
members.how: Honored above all his peers, and forever one step short.

members.name: Francesca da Rimini
members.who: A noblewoman who loved Paolo, her husband's brother.
members.what: Blown forever on the black wind with the carnal, never parted from him.
members.where: Circle II — Lust (Minos judges at the gate).
members.why: 'Love led us to one death' — a kiss, and the book that was their go-between.
members.when: For one moment's surrender, and forever after.
members.how: She tells it so gently that the pilgrim faints for pity.

members.name: Ciacco
members.who: A Florentine remembered for his appetite.
members.what: A soul prone in cold filthy rain, under the claws of Cerberus.
members.why: He made a god of the belly.
members.how: He lifts his face from the mire only to prophesy Florence's coming grief.

members.name: The Hoarders & the Wasters
members.who: Misers and spendthrifts — many of them clergy and popes.
members.what: Two mobs rolling great weights with their chests, clashing forever.
members.where: Circle IV — Greed (Plutus croaks above them).
members.why: They loved getting and spending past all measure.
members.when: Forever, at the point of collision.
members.how: Faces worn featureless, pushing dead weight against the dead weight of the other side.

members.name: Filippo Argenti
members.who: A haughty Florentine, an enemy of Dante in life.
members.what: A wrathful soul in the marsh of the Styx who lunges at the passing boat.
members.where: Circle V — Wrath (Phlegyas ferries; the sullen choke below the mud).
members.why: A rage that could never be governed.
members.when: Drowned forever in his own fury.
members.how: He is torn at by the other wrathful — and Dante, for once, is glad to watch.

members.name: Farinata degli Uberti
members.who: A great Ghibelline leader of Florence.
members.what: A heretic standing upright in a flaming tomb, magnificent even in Hell.
members.where: Circle VI — Heresy, within the iron walls of Dis.
members.why: He denied the soul's immortality, with Epicurus for company.
members.how: He rises from the fire as if it were nothing, to speak of Florence with undimmed pride.

members.name: Pier della Vigna
members.who: A loyal minister of Emperor Frederick II, ruined by envy and slander.
members.what: A suicide, now a thorn-tree in the wood of the self-violent, fed on by Harpies.
members.where: Circle VII — Violence against the self.
members.why: Despair drove him to take the life that was not his alone to take.
members.when: When his honor was stolen, he stole his own death.
members.how: He can speak only when a branch is snapped and the wound of him bleeds and hisses.

members.name: Brunetto Latini
members.what: Among the violent against nature, running forever across the burning sand.
members.where: Circle VII — Violence against God and nature.
members.why: For the sin the poem places him under, though it never stops loving him.
members.when: Under the slow rain of fire.
members.how: He runs beside his old pupil a little way, and is mourned with real tenderness.

members.name: The Minotaur
members.who: The bull-man of Crete, got of an unnatural lust.
members.what: The bellowing warden of the Circle of Violence.
members.where: The shattered cliff down into Circle VII.
members.why: Rage and monstrousness made into a gatekeeper.
members.how: It gnaws itself in fury — and in its blind rage the travelers slip past.

members.name: Ulysses
members.who: The cunning king of Ithaca — Odysseus.
members.what: A false counselor, his soul wrapped in a single tongue of flame.
members.where: Circle VIII — Fraud, the eighth ditch of Malebolge.
members.why: The Trojan Horse, and a lifetime of guile that won and ruined.
members.when: He tells of his last mad voyage, past the world's edge, to the drowning mountain.
members.how: He speaks from inside the fire, and his words are gold and ruin at once.

members.name: Pope Nicholas III
members.who: A pope who sold the offices of the Church.
members.what: A simoniac, planted head-down in stone with his soles on fire.
members.where: Circle VIII — Fraud, the third ditch (simony).
members.why: He made merchandise of holy things.
members.when: Head-down, waiting for the next damned pope to shove him deeper.
members.how: He mistakes Dante for Boniface, come early to take his place.

members.name: The Malebranche
members.who: The demons of the fifth ditch — Malacoda and his hooked crew.
members.what: The barb-forked guards of the barrators in their boiling pitch.
members.why: To torment the grafters — and to lie even to the travelers passing through.
members.when: As Dante and Virgil cross the black pitch.
members.how: With hooks, mockery, and a treachery that nearly traps the poets themselves.

members.name: Count Ugolino
members.who: A Pisan count, betrayed and starved to death in a locked tower with his sons.
members.what: A traitor frozen in the ice, gnawing forever the skull of the archbishop who doomed him.
members.where: Circle IX — Treachery (Antenora, to country and party).
members.why: Betrayal answered with betrayal — and a hunger that outlasts death.
members.when: Locked in the ice, telling the most terrible story in Hell.
members.how: He lifts his mouth from Ruggieri's skull only to speak, then bends again to it.

members.name: Lucifer
members.who: The highest of the angels, fallen — Dis, Satan, the Emperor of the woeful realm.
members.what: Three-faced and six-winged, frozen to the waist at the dead center of the Earth, weeping from six eyes and chewing the three arch-traitors — Judas, Brutus, Cassius — in his three mouths.
members.where: Circle IX — Judecca: the bottom of everything.
members.why: He set himself against his Maker; his own beating wings freeze the lake that holds him fast.
members.how: Not roaring but silent and mechanical — the absolute privation of good. And down his very body the travelers climb, and out, 'to see again the stars.'
